app: UX Lab — Rob Armstrong Formal Verification Review
review_dimensions: Lemma nodes must distinguish proved vs sorry (axiom) vs tainted (transitively contaminated)
Proof obligation context must be visible — what is the system trying to prove?
Formal representation (Lean4 code) must be accessible per lemma
Trust boundaries: edges crossing from proved to sorry/tainted must be visually distinct
Sorry contamination BFS: forward DAG traversal from sorry nodes marks descendants
Inspector panel what-if analysis must show cascade propagation (BROKEN/HOLDS)
GSN (Goal Structuring Notation) alignment for assurance case visualization

interactions.description: Full LemmaGraph — Rob checks node trust status, edge validation, and proof context
interactions.expected: Force-directed SVG graph. Nodes colored by framework (SPARTA=#7c3aed, ATT&CK=#ff4444, D3FEND=#00ff88, NIST=#4a9eff, CWE=#ffaa00). Validated edges solid green, unvalidated edges dashed red. Stats bar: N LEMMAS, N EDGES, N% AVG CONFIDENCE. Node labels readable against dark background.

interactions.description: Edge validation status — Rob checks validated vs unvalidated edge contrast
interactions.expected: Validated edges: solid line, green or high-contrast color, visible against #0b1220 background. Unvalidated edges: dashed line, red or amber, clearly distinct from validated. Edge method labels (subsumes, countered_by, mitigated_by) visible on hover or always shown. Minimum contrast 4.5:1 WCAG AA.

interactions.description: Click a lemma node — should open inspector or show formal details
interactions.expected: Node click triggers inspector panel or detail view. For Rob: must show the lemma's formal representation, proof status (proved/sorry/tainted), connected edges with cascade direction. Ideally exposes Lean4 theorem statement.

interactions.description: Inspector panel — typed parameter editor with what-if analysis
interactions.expected: Panel shows: selected node identity (ID, framework, name), parameter editor with typed controls (bool toggle, float slider, enum dropdown), 'What If?' trigger button. Rob checks: parameter types must match Lean4 predicate signatures. Controls must be meaningful for formal verification (not generic UX sliders).

interactions.description: What-if cascade results — BROKEN/HOLDS status per affected chain
interactions.expected: After what-if trigger: cascade results panel showing affected chains. Each chain: BROKEN (red) or HOLDS (green) status with chain path visualization. Summary: N total, N broken, N held. Rob checks: cascade logic must reflect real predicate evaluation, not cosmetic status.

interactions.description: Evidence Hub pane — Rob checks LemmaGraph + ControlTable composition in context
interactions.expected: Center pane (35% width) shows ControlTable above and LemmaGraph below. Rob: the graph must work IN CONTEXT with the table — selecting a control in the table should highlight related nodes in the graph. Cross-component data flow is the formal verification value proposition.
